006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021import java.math.*;
022import java.util.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
025
026/**
027 * Format-neutral POJO walker that re-emits an arbitrary object graph through a {@link TokenWriter}.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Used by {@link TokenWriter#object(Object)} on every format that supports the cursor-level POJO
031 * bridge: the walker introspects the value (null / scalar / array / collection / map / bean) and
032 * recursively emits {@link TokenWriter#startObject() startObject} / {@link TokenWriter#fieldName fieldName}
033 * / {@link TokenWriter#string string} / {@link TokenWriter#number(long) number} /
034 * {@link TokenWriter#bool bool} / {@link TokenWriter#nil nil} / {@link TokenWriter#binary binary}
035 * events on the calling writer.  No raw bytes are written by the walker; per-format encoding is
036 * the {@link TokenWriter}'s job.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
039 *    <li>Object swaps and {@code @Schema} annotations <em>are</em> applied during the walk via
040 *       {@link MarshallingContext#toBeanMap(Object)} (e.g. {@code java.time} types are emitted as
041 *       ISO-8601 strings).  This is the explicit carve-out from the cursor's "purely structural"
042 *       rule for the {@link TokenWriter#object(Object) object} bridge.  The bean introspection
043 *       uses the <b>live session's</b> {@link MarshallingContext} (threaded through
044 *       {@link Options#context()}) so swaps / bean-filters / locale / timezone honor the same
045 *       per-invocation configuration as {@code serialize(...)}; it falls back to
046 *       {@link MarshallingContext#DEFAULT} only when no context is supplied.
047 *    <li>Self-referencing graphs are guarded by an identity-based ancestor check: re-entering an
048 *       object already on the current walk path throws {@link IllegalStateException} rather than
049 *       recursing until the stack overflows.  (Shared but acyclic references &mdash; the same node
050 *       reachable by two distinct paths &mdash; are permitted.)
051 * </ul>
052 */
053@SuppressWarnings({
054   "resource" // TokenWriter is a fluent Closeable returning itself; discarding the chained return value is intentional, and the writer is the caller's to close.
055})
056public final class PojoWalker {
057
058   /**
059    * Databind-level settings honored by {@link PojoWalker}.
060    *
061    * <p>
062    * Built by the format's {@code serializeTokens(...)} factory from the calling
063    * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder Serializer.Builder} and
064    * passed through to the walker.  These are the same settings the POJO databind path
065    * ({@code Serializer.serialize(...)}) consults; honoring them here means
066    * {@code serializeTokens(out).object(o)} produces the same shape as
067    * {@code serializer.serialize(o)} for the same configuration.
068    *
069    * @param keepNullProperties Whether bean fields with null values are emitted (true) or
070    *    skipped (false).
071    * @param trimEmptyMaps Whether bean / map fields whose value is an empty map are skipped.
072    * @param trimEmptyCollections Whether bean / map fields whose value is an empty collection /
073    *    array are skipped.
074    * @param sortMaps Whether map / bean property iteration order is sorted by key.
075    * @param sortCollections Whether collection element iteration order is sorted (a sorted copy
076    *    is walked).
077    * @param trimStrings Whether emitted scalar strings are trimmed.
078    * @param context The live session's {@link MarshallingContext} used for bean introspection
079    *    (swaps / bean-filters / locale / timezone).  May be <jk>null</jk>, in which case
080    *    {@link MarshallingContext#DEFAULT} is used.
081    */
082   public record Options(
083         boolean keepNullProperties,
084         boolean trimEmptyMaps,
085         boolean trimEmptyCollections,
086         boolean sortMaps,
087         boolean sortCollections,
088         boolean trimStrings,
089         MarshallingContext context) {
090
091      /**
092       * Default options: emit nulls, no trimming, no sorting, no bound context (falls back to
093       * {@link MarshallingContext#DEFAULT}).  Mirrors
094       * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder Serializer.Builder}'s
095       * defaults.
096       */
097      public static final Options DEFAULT = new Options(true, false, false, false, false, false, null);
098
099      /** Returns the bound context, or {@link MarshallingContext#DEFAULT} when none was supplied. */
100      MarshallingContext contextOrDefault() {
101         return context != null ? context : MarshallingContext.DEFAULT;
102      }
103   }
104
105   private PojoWalker() {
106      // Static-method facade.
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Walks the given value and emits its structure through the given writer using
111    * {@link Options#DEFAULT default options}.
112    *
113    * @param w The token writer to emit events to.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
114    * @param value The value to walk.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
115    * @throws IOException If the writer's underlying stream fails.
116    */
117   public static void walk(TokenWriter w, Object value) throws IOException {
118      walk(w, value, Options.DEFAULT);
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Walks the given value and emits its structure through the given writer using the supplied
123    * options.
124    *
125    * @param w The token writer to emit events to.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
126    * @param value The value to walk.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
127    * @param options The walker's databind-level options.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
128    * @throws IOException If the writer's underlying stream fails.
129    */
130   public static void walk(TokenWriter w, Object value, Options options) throws IOException {
131      // Identity-based ancestor set: tracks the objects on the current walk path so a
132      // self-referencing graph throws instead of overflowing the stack.  Shared-but-acyclic
133      // references are fine because each node is removed from the set once its subtree completes.
134      walkValue(w, value, options, Collections.newSetFromMap(new IdentityHashMap<>()));
135   }
136
137   private static void walkValue(TokenWriter w, Object value, Options options, Set<Object> ancestors) throws IOException {
138      if (value == null) {
139         w.nil();
140         return;
141      }
142      if (value instanceof CharSequence cs) {
143         var s = cs.toString();
144         if (options.trimStrings)
145            s = s.trim();
146         w.string(s);
147         return;
148      }
149      if (value instanceof Boolean b) {
150         w.bool(b);
151         return;
152      }
153      if (value instanceof byte[] bytes) {
154         w.binary(bytes);
155         return;
156      }
157      if (value instanceof Number n) {
158         writeNumber(w, n);
159         return;
160      }
161      if (value instanceof Character c) {
162         w.string(c.toString());
163         return;
164      }
165      if (value instanceof Map<?, ?> m) {
166         enterAncestor(value, ancestors);
167         try {
168            walkMap(w, m, options, ancestors);
169         } finally {
170            ancestors.remove(value);
171         }
172         return;
173      }
174      if (value instanceof Collection<?> c) {
175         enterAncestor(value, ancestors);
176         try {
177            walkCollection(w, c, options, ancestors);
178         } finally {
179            ancestors.remove(value);
180         }
181         return;
182      }
183      var cls = value.getClass();
184      if (cls.isArray()) {
185         enterAncestor(value, ancestors);
186         try {
187            walkArray(w, value, options, ancestors);
188         } finally {
189            ancestors.remove(value);
190         }
191         return;
192      }
193      if (value instanceof Iterable<?> it) {
194         enterAncestor(value, ancestors);
195         try {
196            walkIterable(w, it, options, ancestors);
197         } finally {
198            ancestors.remove(value);
199         }
200         return;
201      }
202      if (value instanceof Enum<?> e) {
203         w.string(e.name());
204         return;
205      }
206      // Inspect the type via the live session's MarshallingContext to decide whether to walk it
207      // as a bean or to fall through to its swapped scalar representation.  Known non-bean types
208      // (Date, Calendar, Temporal, Duration, Period, URI/URL, UUID, Class, etc.) cannot be walked
209      // via toBeanMap; emit them as their string form, which is the same form the databind path
210      // produces via DefaultSwaps.
211      var mc = options.contextOrDefault();
212      var cm = mc.getClassMeta(value.getClass());
213      if (cm.isBean()) {
214         enterAncestor(value, ancestors);
215         try {
216            walkMap(w, mc.toBeanMap(value), options, ancestors);
217         } finally {
218            ancestors.remove(value);
219         }
220         return;
221      }
222      // Non-bean fallback: emit as string.  For java.time / Date / UUID / Class etc. the
223      // toString() form is the canonical ISO-8601 / hex / FQN representation.
224      w.string(value.toString());
225   }
226
227   /** Adds the value to the ancestor set, throwing if it is already on the current walk path. */
228   private static void enterAncestor(Object value, Set<Object> ancestors) {
229      if (!ancestors.add(value))
230         throw new IllegalStateException(
231            "Recursion detected while walking a self-referencing object graph.  Offending type: "
232            + value.getClass().getName());
233   }
234
235   private static void writeNumber(TokenWriter w, Number n) throws IOException {
236      if (n instanceof Long l) { w.number(l.longValue()); return; }
237      if (n instanceof Integer i) { w.number(i.longValue()); return; }
238      if (n instanceof Short s) { w.number(s.longValue()); return; }
239      if (n instanceof Byte b) { w.number(b.longValue()); return; }
240      if (n instanceof Double d) { w.number(d.doubleValue()); return; }
241      if (n instanceof Float f) { w.number(f.doubleValue()); return; }
242      if (n instanceof BigDecimal bd) { w.number(bd); return; }
243      if (n instanceof BigInteger bi) { w.number(bi); return; }
244      w.number(n);
245   }
246
247   private static void walkMap(TokenWriter w, Map<?, ?> m, Options options, Set<Object> ancestors) throws IOException {
248      w.startObject();
249      Iterable<? extends Map.Entry<?, ?>> entries = m.entrySet();
250      if (options.sortMaps && !m.isEmpty())
251         entries = sortedEntries(m);
252      for (var entry : entries) {
253         var key = entry.getKey();
254         var value = entry.getValue();
255         if (skipMapEntry(value, options))
256            continue;
257         w.fieldName(key == null ? "null" : key.toString());
258         walkValue(w, value, options, ancestors);
259      }
260      w.endObject();
261   }
262
263   private static List<Map.Entry<Object, Object>> sortedEntries(Map<?, ?> m) {
264      var list = new ArrayList<Map.Entry<Object, Object>>(m.size());
265      for (var entry : m.entrySet())
266         list.add(new AbstractMap.SimpleEntry<>(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue()));
267      list.sort(Comparator.comparing(e -> {
268         var k = e.getKey();
269         return k == null ? "" : k.toString();
270      }));
271      return list;
272   }
273
274   private static boolean skipMapEntry(Object value, Options options) {
275      if (value == null)
276         return !options.keepNullProperties;
277      if (options.trimEmptyMaps && value instanceof Map<?, ?> m && m.isEmpty())
278         return true;
279      if (options.trimEmptyCollections) {
280         if (value instanceof Collection<?> c && c.isEmpty())
281            return true;
282         if (value.getClass().isArray() && Array.getLength(value) == 0)
283            return true;
284      }
285      return false;
286   }
287
288   private static void walkCollection(TokenWriter w, Collection<?> c, Options options, Set<Object> ancestors) throws IOException {
289      w.startArray();
290      Iterable<?> items = c;
291      if (options.sortCollections && !c.isEmpty()) {
292         var copy = new ArrayList<Object>(c);
293         copy.sort(Comparator.comparing(o -> o == null ? "" : o.toString()));
294         items = copy;
295      }
296      for (var item : items)
297         walkValue(w, item, options, ancestors);
298      w.endArray();
299   }
300
301   private static void walkIterable(TokenWriter w, Iterable<?> it, Options options, Set<Object> ancestors) throws IOException {
302      w.startArray();
303      for (var item : it)
304         walkValue(w, item, options, ancestors);
305      w.endArray();
306   }
307
308   private static void walkArray(TokenWriter w, Object array, Options options, Set<Object> ancestors) throws IOException {
309      w.startArray();
310      var len = Array.getLength(array);
311      if (options.sortCollections && len > 0) {
312         var copy = new ArrayList<Object>(len);
313         for (var i = 0; i < len; i++)
314            copy.add(Array.get(array, i));
315         copy.sort(Comparator.comparing(o -> o == null ? "" : o.toString()));
316         for (var item : copy)
317            walkValue(w, item, options, ancestors);
318      } else {
319         for (var i = 0; i < len; i++)
320            walkValue(w, Array.get(array, i), options, ancestors);
321      }
322      w.endArray();
323   }
324}
